codeReview.summary: The widget-ux-improvements feature implements all 5 required UX enhancements correctly and safely. The code follows the project's vanilla JS patterns, maintains the <15KB size constraint (10.5KB gzipped), and includes proper error handling. Manual browser testing verified all features work as expected.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill documentation doesn't mention that cli/assets/spikes.js is embedded at compile time for the serve command, not loaded from the served directory. This caused confusion during testing when updates to widget/spikes.js weren't immediately reflected in serve mode.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'Consider documenting that the serve command embeds spikes.js from cli/assets/ at compile time, not from the served directory.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The widget now returns structured results {success: boolean, reason?: string} from saveSpike(), requiring callers to handle different outcomes. This pattern change is not documented in AGENTS.md widget patterns section.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: widget/spikes.js: saveSpike() returns {success: true}, {success: false, reason: 'duplicate'}, or {success: false, reason: 'quota'}. Both handlePopoverSave() and handleSave() now branch on result.reason.

summary: PASS. The widget-ux-improvements feature successfully implements all 5 requirements: (1) Save confirmation toast with 2.5s duration and auto-dismiss animation, (2) localStorage quota handling with user-friendly error dialog and retry option, (3) Duplicate detection within 30-second window using selector+reviewer+comment comparison, (4) z-index fixed to 2147483647 (max 32-bit signed int) for all widget elements, (5) data-offset-x/y attributes for fine-tuning button position. Widget size is 10.5KB gzipped (under 15KB limit). Manual browser testing verified all behaviors. One non-blocking observation: the serve command's compile-time embedding of spikes.js should be documented in AGENTS.md to prevent future confusion.
